subject: Sociology
title: Can Inequality Be Reduced
description: A 60-second lesson on Can Inequality Be Reduced.

hook.text: Scandinavian countries have half the inequality of the U.S. and higher happiness. The tradeoff isn't what you'd expect.

buildup.text: Progressive taxation, universal healthcare, and free education are proven tools for reducing inequality.

discovery.text: Nordic nations show that lower inequality correlates with better health, less crime, and stronger economic growth.

twist.text: Political will is the barrier, not knowledge. We know what works. Implementing it threatens powerful interests.

climax.text: Reducing inequality is a choice societies make — or refuse to make. The tools exist. The question is power.

punchline.text: We know how to reduce inequality. The question is: who decides?

quiz.question: What do Nordic countries demonstrate about inequality?
options: Lower inequality correlates with better health and growth
High inequality leads to more innovation
